steps.title: Equate coefficients of x
steps.content:
For infinitely many solutions, the coefficients of $x$ on both sides must be equal and the constant terms must be equal.
The given equation is $$(2k+4)x + (3k-14) = (k+32)x + (k+42)$$
Set the $x$‑coefficients equal:
$$2k+4 = k+32$$
Subtract $k$ from both sides:
$$k+4 = 32$$
Subtract $4$:
$$k = 28$$

steps.title: Check constant terms
steps.content:
Now equate the constant terms:
$$3k-14 = k+42$$
Subtract $k$:
$$2k-14 = 42$$
Add $14$:
$$2k = 56$$
Divide by $2$:
$$k = 28$$
Both conditions give the same value, so the equation has infinitely many solutions when $k=28$.
The answer is 28.
